methods.description: Process for collecting sediment cores from Lagunas Reales and preparing them for analysis.

steps.description: In January 2018, we carried a coring campaign at the Lagunas Reales. Nine cores with a portable Ejkelkamp vibracoring system (total recovered length: 14.1 m; diameter: 5 cm) and 4 percussion cores (total recovered length: 17.82 m, diameter: 6.2 cm) were recovered.
steps.searchable_summary: collect sediment cores using Ejkelkamp vibracoring system

steps.description: The cores were divided in two in the IGME laboratories and a half was stored as an archive, whilst the other half was used for description, analyses and sampling.
steps.searchable_summary: split cores for archiving and analysis

steps.description: All of them were photographed, their sedimentary features were described, and stratigraphic logs were elaborated correcting the depths to remove the effect of compaction due to drilling.
steps.searchable_summary: photograph and describe sedimentary features, create stratigraphic logs

methods.description: Non-destructive analyses performed on sediment cores to determine elemental composition and lightness.

steps.description: XRF scanning with a GEOTEK XRF core scanner in a He purged atmosphere with an illumination window of 15 mm (cross-core slit width) × 10 mm (down-core resolution). Two runs, with a 30 s count time exposure, were performed for 10 kV and 40 kV (detecting from Mg to U). XRF spectra were processed with bAxil. Element intensities are represented in peak area.
steps.searchable_summary: perform XRF scanning and process spectra with bAxil

steps.description: Lightness (L*) analyses were performed with 1 cm down core resolution using a Konica Minolta 700-d spectrophotometer integrated in the GEOTEK XRF core scanner. Lightness values range from 0 (black) to 100 (white).
